document.title: Pocket Expense - File Upload
document.module: OOP Pocket Expense CSV Batch Upload

overview.description: CSV-based bulk upload of out-of-pocket expenses for a selected user.
key_capabilities: Admin selects a target user whose expenses will be created
Admin uploads a CSV file following a defined template (max 200 rows)
API performs synchronous validation and creation

validation_behavior.description: Validates file headers and each row against business rules and master data (currencies, expense types, sources, countries)
validation_behavior.on_failure: If any row fails validation, no expenses are created and an error payload is returned
validation_behavior.on_success: If all rows pass validation, the system creates new pocket_expense (+ related metadata) records and returns a success payload

overview.audit: An audit record of each upload is stored in the new table pocket_expense_file_uploads
overview.error_download: The frontend can show a detailed error list and allow users to download errors as CSV

steps: User navigates to Out-of-Pocket Expenses Batch Upload
User selects target user from dropdown (shows only users this admin can manage, or all for primary admin). For Business/Card User context, default to the logged-in user.
User downloads CSV template, fills it, then uploads completed file
System does basic file checks immediately (size, extension etc)

sub_steps: File and selected user info are saved as upload with default status
CSV is parsed and Row level validation validates all entries (synchronous process, all errors captured as array/json and sent back as response)
If all entries are good, the expense records should be created with number of entries and success message
A notification should be issued once all expenses are created for the user

steps.step: For Error screen User can review result
sub_steps: Each line of error should be displayed for the user with line number, error field and error reason
This list should be downloadable as CSV for user to keep as a future reference

file_constraints.header_row: mandatory and must exactly match the column names

validation_rules: One of: ATM Withdrawal, Point of Sale, Fee & Charges, Refund from Merchant
+ve or -ve amount validation based on type selection
Add sign +ve or -ve based on type selection
Add sign +ve or -ve based on type selection
If present, must be numeric between 0-100 (after stripping %)
Max length check per DB definition

columns.storage_note: For the client_id, store at pocket_expense_metadata
validation_rules: When 'Other', Source Note must be filled
Optional but Required when Source = Other
Follow DB limit, trim and prevent SQL injection

new_table.name: pocket_expense_file_uploads
new_table.model_notes: Standard Eloquent model using soft deletes

form_fields.name: user_id
form_fields.description: Current authenticated admin user (from token / context)

form_fields.name: expense_user_id
form_fields.description: Target user for whom expenses are created

additional_checks: Header row presence and exact column names
user_id (current admin) has permission to manage expense_user_id

steps.name: Initial validation & upload record
details: CSV is stored on disk (e.g. storage/app/pocket-expense-uploads)
A PocketExpenseFileUpload row is created with status='uploaded', user_id=expense_user_id, client_id, created_by_user_id, file_name, file_path, uploaded_at

steps.name: CSV parsing & row-level validation
details: PocketExpenseCSVValidator service is instantiated with targetUserId, clientId, adminId
Parses CSV into rows (excluding header) max 200 rows
Iterates each row, running datatype and cross-field validations
Accumulates errors: array of structured error objects
All-or-nothing: Either all valid rows succeed for storage of pocket expense or nothing

steps.name: Validation result persistence
details: PocketExpenseFileUpload record is updated with total_records, valid_records, validation_errors (JSON array), validated_at
status='validation_failed' if any errors, otherwise remains ready to proceed

actions: API responds with 422 and error payload
Create pocket_expense record for each row with associated metadata (e.g. expense source, possibly VAT / additional info)
Update upload record: status='completed', processed_at set

steps.name: Notification
steps.details: After successful creation of all expenses, issue notification to the target user/admin (channel TBD: in-app and/or email)

validation_service.name: PocketExpenseCSVValidator

preloaded_reference_data.data: Expense sources configured for the client

validation_service.caching: Implement caching if possible for dropdown values

Transaction Type.sign_note: Determines sign of Amount (e.g. Refund from Merchant vs regular expense)

Amount.sign_conversion: Converted to signed value based on Transaction Type (refund vs expense)

Source.conditional: If Source = Other, then Source Note is required and length-validated

Merchant Country.must_match: allowed country list (same logic as Volopa API)

validation_service.row_failure_behavior: If any check fails for a row, that row is marked invalid and contributes to the errors list

notes: line_number corresponds to CSV line (including header), i.e. header = line 1
errors is also stored in pocket_expense_file_uploads.validation_errors for later retrieval/download
frontend_requirements: Display each error row with: Line number, Field name, Error reason
Provide a 'Download errors as CSV' option by re-formatting the errors array into a CSV for user reference

items.question: How to display wallet base currency for file upload?
items.answer: A new optional field to capture data, this can be edited in the edit form

items.question: How to capture all metadata for file uploaded expenses?
items.answer: This will be added from the edit form

items.question: How could receipt be attached for each expense?
items.answer: This will be added from the edit form

items.question: Where is the menu / page placed for file upload?
Will there be any notification for approval when file upload completed by regular user?
items.answer: Notification for user when file upload has created all the expenses

items.question: What is the expected maximum number of expense?

flow: 1. Validate CSV -> If errors -> Return errors (stop)
2. If no errors -> Store in local table (committed)
5. Background job syncs to main service in batches (batch of 100)

bulk_insert_pattern.description: Bulk insert to local table with single query for performance
process: Build array of data from validation result valid_rows
Update file upload table status to 'processing'

server_side_checks: Current user must have permission to manage expense_user_id (same rules as existing expense tooling)
Client must have Out-of-Pocket feature enabled
